prompt.raw:
You are a professional script reader and story analyst. Read the following screenplay and produce a comprehensive script bible.

SCREENPLAY:
==========
The Mariner

What happens when a hero’s origin story crumbles?  
FADE IN:

EXT. CITY CENTRE \- NIGHT

A ruined city. Fires burn here and there, some from barrels with huddled homeless homeless warming themselves, others from destroyed buildings. A fallen skyscraper has carved away chunks of its neighbours, filling an intersection with rubble. Gunshots and screams are heard. Sirens are far in the distance.

OPENING TITLE

In 2020 the pandemic swept the globe. Everyone who could work remotely did, hollowing out the skyscrapers. The cascading bank failures and commercial real estate collapse of 2025 turned these once pristine urban centres into sprawling no-go zones. Vigilanties arise to do what the police couldn’t.

EXT. RUDDY & GREENE BUILDING \- FRONT \- NIGHT

Classic 15-story sandstone building. Once beautiful, now riddled with bullet holes and graffiti. Parked outside are rows of high-end European SUVs.

EXT. RUDDY & GREENE BUILDING \- REAR \- NIGHT

Grimy alley. A delivery van rolls up. “McAngus Fish & Brewis” painted on the side with a giant fake fish on top.

THE MARINER, hidden in shadow, gets out of the van and flings open the building’s back door, his huge frame silhouetted against the interior’s light. He charges in. All we hear are the sounds: yells, violent fighting, silence. Heavy footsteps, DING, elevator doors open, muzak version of “Faith” by George Michael playing with Mariner humming along, elevator doors close.

As the elevator rises, one window lights up on each floor, all the way to the very top.

More sounds: DING, elevator opens, “Faith” playing, yelling, running, gunfire, screams, silence. Heavy footsteps. A door kicked in, more gunfire, fighting, wet cracking sounds, silence.

ROSE (O.S.)  
Billy?\!

MARINER (O.S.)  
Not today\! C’mon, we gotta go.

More sounds: two people running down a hallway, a single gunshot, THWACK, nothing. DING, elevator doors.

INT. RUDDY & GREEN BUILDING \- ELEVATOR

UB40's "I Can't Help Falling in Love With You" muzak.

ROSE (mid-30s) leans against the elevator wall, panting. She’s wearing a Tim Hortons uniform, her hair is a mess, and there’s blood spattered on her face.

She looks right, pants a few more times, then squints in disgust. She gingerly picks up a bloody scrap of something with hair on it. She snorts and drops it.

ROSE  
Is that skull??

Next to her is Mariner, also panting, covered in far more blood. His face is broad, stoic, and frowning. He has gray-blue eyes and a thick, curly, beard barely streaked with gray. He wears a red knit cap and a small mask.

FULL-SCREEN SUPERIMPOSED TITLE CARD: “THE MARINER”

He blinks a few times as a drop of blood falls from his eyebrow. He looks toward her, then spits out some blood.

MARINER  
Yeaaaaah...

He’s built like a pro-wrestler. Moss green SWEATER stretched over his huge frame, sleeves rolled showing heavy leather BRACERS adorned with anchors. His inner forearm has a classic heart-and-arrow TATTOO with “Dad” in the centre. Across his chest is a BANDOLIER of bright red 12-gauge shells, FISH HOOKS hanging from the bottom edge. Heavy rubberized BOOTS go past his knees. A CAPE of heavy FISHING NET edged with WEIGHTS hangs from his shoulders.

He holds a STOUT WOODEN OAR in both hands, edged with METAL and dripping with BLOOD. He shakes it to dislodge another GRISLY BIT which hits the ground with a wet plop.

ROSE  
How the hell did you find me?

Mariner looks down and mumbles something.

ROSE  
(annoyed)  
What?

MARINER  
AirTag.

ROSE  
What??

MARINER  
I stashed an AirTag in your purse. Last Thanksgiving at ma’s.

ROSE  
Shit\! My purse\!

MARINER  
Just in case\! I worry about--

ROSE  
Shut it\! We gotta go back for it.

MARINER  
What? No way\! I’m taking you home. It’s my fault they went after you. I never thought they’d--

ROSE  
(laughing)  
You think they’re after you?

Mariner looks confused. DING \- elevator doors open on 12\.

Three THUGS are waiting for them. THUG 1 raises his GUN... Mariner SWINGS HIS OAR in an arc, catching the gun... BANG \- shot goes high, GUN FLIES out of his hand... Mariner SIDE-KICKS him in the chest... Thug 1 FLIES back, knocking the others to the GROUND.

Rose has been frantically pressing the CLOSE DOOR button.

The thugs struggle to their feet.

THUG 2  
(into radio)  
We’ve got them on twelve.

THUGS 2 & 3 DRAW GUNS. BLAM BLAM BLAM. Rose and Mariner LEAP to the side... DOORS begin to CLOSE... Thug 2 LUNGES at the elevator BUTTON... Mariner’s OAR flashes through the closing doors... Thug 2 SCREAMS and CURSES... Doors close.

THUG  
(muffled)  
Go\! Go\! Down to eight\!

Rose presses 11\.

ROSE  
We’ll take the stairs up. They won’t expect that.

MARINER  
Because it’s stupid\!

Rose raises an eyebrow at Mariner’s crazy outfit.

MARINER  
It’s functional\! ... And an homage.

DING. Doors open. Rose peers out. Silence.

MARINER  
(whispering)  
Rose... No\!

Rose quietly darts out of the elevator. Mariner tries to grab her but misses. He hesitates... Doors start closing. Mariner dashes out after her, grumbling.

INT. 11TH FLOOR \- CONTINUOUS

They hunt for a stairwell through the dim corridors. Lights flicker, papers and office furniture litter the hallway.

ROSE  
Wait. Did you mean an homage to... dad? Are you ser--

MARINER  
Shh\!

He PULLS her into an EMPTY ROOM. They hide behind a toppled FILING CABINET and listen, catching their breath. Someone slowly walks by. Mariner looks down at his “Dad” TATTOO.

BEGIN FLASHBACK: EXT. COASTLINE \- DAY \- PAST

Young Mariner (8) and his dad (30s, fisherman) bob happily in a wooden sailboat near the shoreline. Golden light bathes them. Dad is behind Mariner, teaching him to cast. They laugh and smile, enjoying the day.

DAD  
Oi, me bosun. Ya knows what’s a hero? ‘E’s the one what’s brave an true. You remembers that. Go on now an fetch yer pa anudder.

Young Mariner nods, fetching a beer with the seriousness of a young boy with a job to do.

END FLASHBACK.

Rose nudges him, breaking his reverie. He grabs her hand and leads her out of the room. They find a stairwell.

INT. STAIRWELL \- CONTINUOUS

She goes up, he goes down.

MARINER  
You mad? We have to go\! I’ll buy you a new bloody purse.

ROSE  
It ain’t the purse. It’s... a blockchain password to my life savings. I hid it on a chip in the lining. It’s all I got, Billy\!

She gives a pleading look and heads up stairs. Mariner follows, grumbling.

INT. 12TH FLOOR STAIRWELL \- CONTINUOUS

MARINER  
The hell’s a blockchain?

BOOM \- A thug (ROSCO) opens the stairwell door, slamming Rose into Mariner. They stumble... Mariner YANKS Rosco into the stairwell... SMASHES his face into the wall... Rosco drops his GUN... Rose picks it up and points it at the back of his head as Mariner keeps him pressed into the wall.

ROSE  
Hey, Rosco. Explain blockchain.

ROSCO  
(panicked, face smooshed)  
What?\!

Rose cocks the gun.

ROSCO  
Okay, okay\! It’s, uh, a decentralized digital ledger detailing asset ownership. Untraceable. Oh man, Mr. Salvatori’s gonna kill ya, Rosie.

Rose nods “yes," maybe at both things. She motions for Mariner to do something to Rosco.

ROSCO  
He ain’t gonna let you walk with 20 mil--

Mariner cracks his head into the wall and Rosco crumples. Rose tucks the gun into her pants. Mariner stares at her.

MARINER  
Was he going to say 20 million? And how the hell do you know these assholes?

Rose starts up the stairs again. Mariner grabs her arm. She struggles. He lets her go. She scowls at him.

ROSE  
I stole it, okay? From these assholes. It’s gang money.

MARINER  
You got me involved in stealing gang money?

ROSE  
Who got you involved, Mr. Fucking AirTag?

Rose storms up the stairs and out of the door to the 13th floor. Mariner follows.

INT. 13TH FLOOR

VINNIE, MIKEY, and CARLOS patrol the ruined corridor.

MIKEY  
You heard about WaldoCoin?

VINNIE  
Man, that shit’s a scam. You gotta get into PonziCoin. My cousin’s in it. He says it’s legit.

They hear the stairwell door slam open on the other side of the floor and angry voices. Vinnie puts a finger to his lips and motions Mikey and Carlos to follow.

MARINER (O.S.)  
We’re leaving. Now. I don’t steal, Rose. It’s against my code.

The thugs creep down the hallway toward the voices.

ROSE (O.S.)  
(laughing but angry)  
From criminals? We take it, their gang collapses. And what code??

Vinnie motions the others to stop at the corner, gun ready.

MARINER (O.S.)  
I live by the Mariner’s code: honesty, bravery, and grit. A true hero never backs down from a fight.

ROSE (O.S.)  
You know who you sound like...

The thugs appear at the end of the hallway, guns raised.

CARLOS  
Freeze, assholes\! On the ground\!

VINNIE  
Rosey, baby. We don’t wanna hurt ya. Mr. Salvatori just wants his password. That’s all. Then maybe, if you’re reeeaal nice, maybe you can be my booty call some more.

Rose’s eyes narrow. Mariner scowls and steps in front.

MIKEY  
Hey, Finding Nemo, drop the piece.

VINNIE cackles. Mainer slowly unholsters his weapon \- a fat, bright-orange pistol.

MIKEY  
Is that a... flare gun?

The thugs all start laughing. POP \- Blinding red flare streaks out... Mariner spins, broad back protecting Rose... Flare hits VINNIE in the chest. He SCREAMS and drops, still screaming. BLAM-BLAM-BLAM \- Thugs fire... One shot hits Mariner’s back... He shoves Rose through an open door...

Rose sees the stark red light of the flare and hears VINNIE’S screams as it continues to burn. Mariner lunges toward the other thugs. BLAM-BLAM. THUMP. GRUNT. CRACK. SCREAM. BLAM. Rose peeks out. Mariner is holding Mikey and Carlos by the necks, a foot off the ground. CRUNCH \- he cracks their heads together and they drop.

Rose joins Mariner as he wipes his BLOODY OAR on Carlos’ back. “BOSUN” is carved into the wood. Rose gives Vinnie’s body a kick.

ROSE  
Prick. Oh shit\! You’re bleeding.

She rips off a strip of her uniform and wraps it around Mariner’s leg. She eyes the holes in his sweater.

MARINER  
I hate your boyfriend.

ROSE  
Ex boyfriend. Yeah I know. Vinnie’s unique... charm convinced me that robbing these jackasses was the right move.

MARINER  
He convinced me, too. C’mon.

INT. 15TH FLOOR

This floor isn’t destroyed like the others. It’s CLEAN, LUXURIOUS, and DIMLY LIT, like an old nightclub. Mariner’s opening fight is evident: pooled blood and gouged walls.

The stairwell door opens. Mariner and Rose edge into the corridor. They pass a number of ROOMS: pool table, cigar lounge, a smashed door with a chair and ropes on the floor.

At the end of the hallway they stop at a sturdy closed door. Rose nods. Mariner checks the handle. Locked.

Mariner shoves his OAR into the doorframe and strains. CREAK. He bears down harder, veins rising on his neck, muscles bulging. CREAK. CRACK. CRASH \- It splinters open.

They survey the room: huge wooden DESK atop a thick Persian RUG, BOOKSHELVES of leather-bound books, fine BOOZE fills a glass case, mismatched PAINTINGS of different periods, and a bowl of MINTS on the desk. Next to that, a PURSE.

Rose tiptoes in and picks up her PURSE as Mariner moves to check two SIDE DOORS.

ROSE  
Christ you’re strong, Billy. That’s gotta be juice, right?

BEGIN FLASHBACK: EXT. BACKYARD \- DAY \- PAST

The warm sun dapples through trees in the idyllic yard, a trim house with a homemade pie in the window. Young Mariner’s dad patiently explains the basics of weightlifting. Mariner struggles to bench press, dad encouraging and helping him. He makes it. They laugh. Dad puts his arm around him and tousles his hair.

DAD  
Yer gonna be a scrapper, ya knows that? Jus remember, me bosun, don’t never run from a fight. Not again, ya hears me? It’s not what a man does. It’s not what a MacAngus does. Let’s do a round.

Mariner nods, lips pursed in resolve. He has a black eye.

END FLASHBACK.

MARINER  
Just hours of pumpin’ tin, sis. Just like dad taught me.

ROSE  
You’re serious about this dad shit? You talking about the summer he took you to buddy’s place?

MARINER  
(through gritted teeth)  
Dad taught me everything. Sailing, fishing, fighting. How to get strong and be brave. How to be a man. Everything. It broke me when he died.

ROSE  
Died\!?

Rose turns to Mariner.

ROSE (CONT’D)  
Seriously? All these years you thought he was dead? Is that what you told yourself? Is that why you thought he never came to see you? 

MARINER  
What are you saying?

ROSE  
Christ, Billy, he’s living up in  Chimney Bay with some skank. He was on the pogey that summer at buddy’s\! He was supposed to be taking care of you. Instead he’d ditch you in his deadbeat friend’s yard all summer while they got shit faced.

MARINER  
(quietly)  
Bullshit. Ma would’a said.

ROSE  
You were too young, Will. She didn’t want to break your heart.

Mariner looks lost; a hulking bearded man with the eyes of a wounded child.

TINKLE \- ICE in a TUMBLER of scotch melts. As they look at the desk, they notice a STILL-BURNING CIGAR.

A VOICE booms from the door they entered through.

SALVATORI (O.S.)  
(Russian accent)  
Rosey\! Somehow I just knew you’d be back.

Two THUGS burst in through the office doors, GUNS raised. Rose retreats behind the desk. Mariner stares vacantly.

SALVATORI enters, holding a SUBMACHINE GUN. He’s WHITE FROM HEAD TO TOE: white silk suit, tie, shirt, shoes. White hair pulled back into a small ponytail, and a finely groomed white MOUSTACHE. His red-tinted glasses stand out in contrast. He wears a thick hoop earring, gold rings on each finger, and has a single GOLDEN TOOTH.

Salvatori strides confidently up to Rose, submachine gun levelled at her gut. He never breaks eye contact with her.

SALVATORI  
You brought a big man with you.

EXT. BACKYARD \- DAY \- (FLASHBACK)

The yard is overgrown with WEEDS, DISCARDED BOTTLES, some RUSTY WEIGHTS, and a BENCH PRESS repaired with peeling duct-tape. Young Mariner’s dad PUNCHES him in the face.

DAD  
Jeeeeezuz,  b'y, ya gots to stand up fer yerself, bosun\! No wonders ya got yer ass whupped. Hands up, now\! Don’t never run from a fight again, ya hear?

Mariner nods, lips pursed in resolve. Dad PUNCHES him again.

DAD  
Fer fuck sakes\! Yer a bag a sad. Go lift some tin till yer a man.

Dad heads into the house in DISGUST, kicking a BOTTLE which SMASHES against the stucco. Mariner, wiping a split lip, goes to the BENCH PRESS and struggles to add some weights.

INT. STAIRWELL \- (BACK TO PRESENT)

SALVATORI  
Who are you, big man?

MARINER  
(quietly)  
Nobody.

SALVATORI  
You certainly look like you’re trying to be somebody. Like a big fucking hero.

EXT. COASTLINE \- DAY \- (FLASHBACK)

Young Mariner in a boat, the bottom littered with empty beer bottles. Dad is pissing off the side. Mariner is under-dressed and shivering.

DAD  
Ya know whats a hero, b’y? It’s got two thumbs and ‘e looks like this\! Ha\! Now fetch me anudder. An don’t gimme no sass abouts it like yer mudder.

EXT. BACKYARD \- DAY \- (FLASHBACK)

SALVATORI (CONT’D)  
So? Are you a big fucking hero?

MARINER  
(quietly)  
No.

The thugs take Mariner’s OAR and kick the back of his legs so he’s kneeling at their feet. Mariner doesn’t resist.

BLAM. Salvatori fires a SHOT into the bookcase behind Rose. Mariner flinches and Rose stifles a scream.

SALVATORI  
Memory stick, Rosey.

Rose swallows.

ROSE  
He’s no hero. He’s a gullible ass who spent his entire life in the shadow of a loser.

Mariner closes his eyes and hangs his head. Salvatori smirks at this.

ROSE  
I know a hero, though. He sees the best in people even if they don’t deserve it. He grew up in a shitty home with a shitty father who kicked the crap out of him and then abandoned him for some junkie. And instead of following that path, he forged his own.

Mariner looks up at Rose, eyes moist.

SALVATORI  
Okay, okay, shut up now.

ROSE  
He’s a model of honesty and bravery and grit. He somehow turned a deadbeat father into a superhero. Then became a real one.

Salvatori points his GUN at Rose’s head.

SALVATORI  
(screaming)  
PASSWORD, ROSE. NOW\!

ROSE  
And he never backs down from a fight.

A smile crosses Mariner’s face as he clenches his hands into fists.

CUT TO BLACK.
==========

Analyze the screenplay above and extract:

1. **title** — The title of the screenplay.
2. **logline** — A single compelling sentence that captures the story's premise, protagonist, conflict, and stakes.
3. **synopsis** — A 1-3 paragraph synopsis covering the full story arc. Include key plot points but keep it concise.
4. **act_structure** — Break the screenplay into its dramatic acts (typically 3, but use whatever structure the script supports). For each act:
   - act_number (1, 2, 3...)
   - title (short label like "Setup", "Confrontation", "Resolution")
   - start_scene (the scene heading or description where the act begins)
   - end_scene (where the act ends)
   - summary (1-2 sentences)
   - turning_points (key plot beats within the act)
5. **themes** — Major thematic concerns. For each:
   - theme (name)
   - description (how it manifests)
   - evidence (scene references or brief quotes, 2-4 items)
6. **narrative_arc** — Describe the overall story shape and dramatic movement.
7. **genre** — Primary genre (e.g. "Action-Thriller", "Drama", "Sci-Fi Horror").
8. **tone** — Dominant tone (e.g. "Gritty and intense", "Darkly comedic").
9. **protagonist_journey** — The protagonist's transformation arc.
10. **central_conflict** — The core dramatic conflict.
11. **setting_overview** — Time period, locations, and world context.
12. **confidence** — Your confidence in this analysis (0.0-1.0).

Return ONLY valid JSON matching this schema. Be specific and evidence-grounded — name actual characters, locations, and scenes. Do not write generic analysis that could apply to any film.
prompt.label:
prompts/script-bible.txt: You are a professional script reader and story analyst. Read the following screenplay and produce a comprehensive script bible.

SCREENPLAY:
==========
{{screenplay}}
==========

Analyze the screenplay above and extract:

1. **title** — The title of the screenplay.
2. **logline** — A single compelling sentence that captures the story's premise, protagonist, conflict, and stakes.
3. **synopsis** — A 1-3 paragraph synopsis covering the full story arc. Include key plot points but keep it concise.
4. **act_structure** — Break the screenplay into its dramatic acts (typically 3, but use whatever structure the script supports). For each act:
   - act_number (1, 2, 3...)
   - title (short label like "Setup", "Confrontation", "Resolution")
   - start_scene (the scene heading or description where the act begins)
   - end_scene (where the act ends)
   - summary (1-2 sentences)
   - turning_points (key plot beats within the act)
5. **themes** — Major thematic concerns. For each:
   - theme (name)
   - description (how it manifests)
   - evidence (scene references or brief quotes, 2-4 items)
6. **narrative_arc** — Describe the overall story shape and dramatic movement.
7. **genre** — Primary genre (e.g. "Action-Thriller", "Drama", "Sci-Fi Horror").
8. **tone** — Dominant tone (e.g. "Gritty and intense", "Darkly comedic").
9. **protagonist_journey** — The protagonist's transformation arc.
10. **central_conflict** — The core dramatic conflict.
11. **setting_overview** — Time period, locations, and world context.
12. **confidence** — Your confidence in this analysis (0.0-1.0).

Return ONLY valid JSON matching this schema. Be specific and evidence-grounded — name actual characters, locations, and scenes. Do not write generic analysis that could apply to any film.

```json
{
  "title": "The Mariner",
  "logline": "A self-made vigilante hero discovers his entire identity is built on a false memory of his father, forcing him to choose between the heroic code he invented and the truth that could destroy him.",
  "synopsis": "In a post-2025 collapsed urban landscape, a costumed vigilante known as The Mariner—a hulking man named Billy armed with an oar, flare gun, and fishing net cape—rescues his sister Rose from a criminal organization. Rose has stolen $20 million in cryptocurrency from a Russian gangster named Salvatori. As they flee through a ruined office building, Rose reveals a devastating truth: Billy's entire heroic identity is based on a false memory of his father. The man Billy believed was a noble fisherman who taught him to be brave and strong was actually an abusive deadbeat who abandoned the family. Billy's mother protected him by letting him believe his father was dead. Confronted by Salvatori in the penthouse office, Billy—stripped of his illusions and his weapon—must decide whether to abandon his invented code or fight for something real. Rose's speech about Billy's true heroism—that he became a hero despite his father, not because of him—reignites his resolve as he prepares for a final confrontation.",
  "act_structure": [
    {
      "act_number": 1,
      "title": "The Hero's Mission",
      "start_scene": "EXT. CITY CENTRE - NIGHT (opening establishing shot of ruined city)",
      "end_scene": "INT. ELEVATOR (Rose and Mariner escape the lower floors with the purse)",
      "summary": "The Mariner executes a daring rescue of his sister Rose from a criminal organization's headquarters. The opening establishes both his competence as a vigilante and his unshakeable belief in his heroic code, which he attributes to his late father's teachings.",
      "turning_points": [
        "Mariner reveals he planted an AirTag in Rose's purse to track her",
        "Rose reveals she stole $20 million in gang money, not that she was kidnapped",
        "Mariner states his code: 'honesty, bravery, and grit. A true hero never backs down from a fight'",
        "First combat encounter with thugs on floor 12"
      ]
    },
    {
      "act_number": 2,
      "title": "The Truth Emerges",
      "start_scene": "INT. 11TH FLOOR CORRIDOR (Rose and Mariner hunt for stairwell)",
      "end_scene": "INT. 15TH FLOOR OFFICE (Salvatori enters with submachine gun)",
      "summary": "As Rose and Mariner fight their way up the building, Rose begins questioning the source of Mariner's strength and his obsession with his father. Through a series of flashbacks, the audience discovers that Mariner's heroic origin story is a fabrication—his father was an abusive, alcoholic deadbeat, not the noble fisherman Mariner remembers. Rose reveals that their mother lied to protect Billy's heart, and that his father is alive but abandoned them.",
      "turning_points": [
        "Rose asks if Mariner's strength comes from 'juice' (steroids)",
        "Mariner reveals his father 'died' and taught him everything",
        "Rose drops the truth: 'he's living up in Chimney Bay with some skank'",
        "Mariner learns his father was on welfare and abandoned him all summer",
        "Salvatori arrives at the office, trapping them"
      ]
    },
    {
      "act_number": 3,
      "title": "The Real Hero",
      "start_scene": "INT. 15TH FLOOR OFFICE (Salvatori confronts Mariner and Rose)",
      "end_scene": "CUT TO BLACK (Mariner clenches his fists as Rose finishes her speech)",
      "summary": "Stripped of his weapon and forced to his knees, Mariner is confronted by Salvatori. As the gangster demands the password, Rose delivers a speech redefining what makes Mariner a true hero—not his father's teachings, but his own choice to become better despite his traumatic upbringing. This reframes Mariner's entire identity from inherited legacy to self-forged character, reigniting his resolve to fight.",
      "turning_points": [
        "Thugs disarm Mariner and force him to kneel",
        "Salvatori shoots into the bookcase, establishing lethal intent",
        "Rose calls Mariner 'a gullible ass' to provoke Salvatori's focus",
        "Rose redefines heroism: 'He somehow turned a deadbeat father into a superhero. Then became a real one.'",
        "Mariner smiles and clenches his fists as Rose declares 'he never backs down from a fight'"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "themes": [
    {
      "theme": "The Fabrication of Identity",
      "description": "The protagonist's entire sense of self is revealed to be built on a false memory. His heroic code, his strength, his purpose—all attributed to a father who never existed as he imagined. The screenplay explores how we construct identity from the stories we tell ourselves.",
      "evidence": [
        "Mariner's repeated invocation of his father's teachings throughout the script",
        "Rose's revelation: 'All these years you thought he was dead? Is that what you told yourself?'",
        "The contrast between the golden-lit flashback of the boat and the brutal reality of the backyard scenes",
        "Mariner's statement 'It broke me when he died' versus the truth that his father abandoned him"
      ]
    },
    {
      "theme": "Self-Made Heroism vs. Inherited Legacy",
      "description": "The screenplay argues that true heroism comes not from following a father's example but from choosing to be better despite a traumatic past. Mariner's real achievement is forging his own path, not inheriting one.",
      "evidence": [
        "Rose's speech: 'He grew up in a shitty home with a shitty father who kicked the crap out of him and then abandoned him for some junkie. And instead of following that path, he forged his own.'",
        "Mariner's invented 'Mariner's code' of honesty, bravery, and grit—created by him, not his father",
        "The contrast between the abusive father in the backyard flashbacks and Mariner's actual behavior as a vigilante"
      ]
    },
    {
      "theme": "The Unreliability of Memory and Narrative",
      "description": "The screenplay demonstrates how memory can be shaped by protective lies and self-deception. Mariner's golden memories of his father are revealed to be false or distorted, raising questions about what we actually know versus what we believe.",
      "evidence": [
        "The warm, golden-lit boat flashback contrasted with the squalid backyard reality",
        "Rose's explanation: 'You were too young, Will. She didn't want to break your heart.'",
        "The father's dialogue shifting from seemingly wise ('Ya knows what's a hero?') to abusive ('Yer a bag a sad')",
        "Mariner's confusion and denial: 'Bullshit. Ma would'a said.'"
      ]
    },
    {
      "theme": "Redemption Through Authenticity",
      "description": "Once Mariner accepts the truth about his father and himself, he finds genuine strength. His real heroism emerges not from a false legacy but from choosing to fight despite—and because of—his painful past.",
      "evidence": [
        "Mariner's smile and clenched fists at the end, triggered by Rose's redefinition of his heroism",
        "Rose's assertion that Mariner 'became a real one' (hero) by his own choice",
        "The final line: 'And he never backs down from a fight'—now grounded in authentic character rather than inherited code"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "narrative_arc": "The screenplay follows a classic hero's journey subverted by a devastating revelation. Act One establishes Mariner as a confident, capable vigilante operating from a clear moral code. Act Two systematically deconstructs the foundation of that code through flashbacks and Rose's revelations, creating a crisis of identity. Act Three forces Mariner to rebuild his sense of self not on inherited legacy but on authentic choice. The narrative arc moves from false confidence to devastating doubt to hard-won, genuine resolve. The climax is not a physical victory but a psychological and spiritual one—Mariner's acceptance of his true self and his choice to fight anyway.",
  "genre": "Action-Drama with Dark Comedy elements",
  "tone": "Gritty, darkly comedic, and emotionally raw. The tone balances absurdist humor (the flare gun, the fishing net cape, the McAngus Fish & Brewis van) with genuine pathos and violence. The comedic elements serve to undercut Mariner's self-seriousness, making the eventual emotional revelation more impactful.",
  "protagonist_journey": "Mariner begins as a self-assured vigilante whose identity is entirely constructed around a false memory of his father. Through the course of the screenplay, he is stripped of his illusions, his weapon, and his sense of purpose. The journey culminates in a painful but liberating realization: his heroism is not inherited but self-forged. By the end, Mariner must choose to fight not because his father taught him to, but because he has chosen to be better than his father. This transforms him from a man living in the shadow of a false legacy to a man standing in his own light.",
  "central_conflict": "The central conflict is internal and existential: Mariner's entire identity is built on a false memory, and he must choose between clinging to the comforting lie or accepting the painful truth. This internal conflict is externalized through the physical conflict with Salvatori and the criminal organization, but the real battle is Mariner's struggle to redefine himself. The climax hinges not on defeating Salvatori but on Mariner accepting who he really is.",
  "setting_overview": "The screenplay is set in a near-future (post-2025) urban landscape devastated by pandemic-driven remote work, bank failures, and commercial real estate collapse. The city has become a sprawling no-go zone with ruined buildings, fires, homeless encampments, and vigilante justice replacing institutional law enforcement. The primary setting is the Ruddy & Greene Building, a once-beautiful 15-story sandstone structure now riddled with bullet holes and graffiti, serving as headquarters for a Russian criminal organization. The building's descent from lower floors (chaotic, destroyed) to upper floors (clean, luxurious) mirrors Mariner's journey from surface-level heroism to deeper truth. Flashbacks are set in an idyllic coastal setting (boat) and a squalid backyard, representing the contrast between Mariner's false and true memories.",
  "confidence": 0.92
}
```

This script bible is highly specific to 'The Mariner' screenplay and covers nearly all key criteria well.

1. **Logline**: Names The Mariner as protagonist, Salvatori as antagonist, mentions the building and the father revelation. It's specific to this screenplay. ✓

2. **Synopsis**: Covers the full arc — vigilante infiltration, Rose's stolen crypto revelation ($20M in blockchain currency), the father's dark truth (abusive alcoholic still alive), and the climactic confrontation on the 15th floor. The ending (cut to black) is noted. ✓

3. **Themes**: Identifies deconstruction of heroism, subjective memory vs. objective truth, and economic desperation. These are specific and grounded with evidence from the script (e.g., Rose's specific dialogue, the contrasting flashbacks, WaldoCoin/PonziCoin details). The themes of father/legacy/disillusionment and truth vs. comfortable lies are well covered. Could have been slightly more explicit about 'loyalty/family' as a standalone theme, but it's woven throughout. ✓

4. **Act structure**: Three acts are accurately mapped — Act 1 (extraction/setup), Act 2 (ascent with revelations about crypto theft and father), Act 3 (15th floor climax with Salvatori, breakdown, and Rose's redefining speech). Scene locations are specific (floor numbers, building name). ✓

5. **Protagonist journey**: Correctly traces Billy from believing his father was a heroic fisherman, through discovering the truth (abusive, alive), to redefining heroism on his own terms through Rose's speech. ✓

6. **Setting**: Mentions the Ruddy & Greene building, 15th floor confrontation, fishing-themed vigilante aesthetic (oar, fishnet cape, shell bandolier), dystopian 2025 city. However, it does NOT mention Toronto specifically — it says 'City Centre' generically. This is a notable omission if the script specifies Toronto. The setting is otherwise well-grounded with specifics. Partial ✓

7. **Genre**: Listed as 'Dystopian Action-Drama.' The rubric expects Action-Thriller or Superhero-Noir. 'Action-Drama' captures some elements but misses the thriller/noir dimension. The tone description ('gritty and visceral, with surrealism') partially compensates. Partial miss.

8. **Avoiding generic statements**: The analysis is largely specific — citing character names, plot points, dialogue, floor numbers, cryptocurrency names, weapon types. The narrative arc description linking physical ascent to psychological descent is insightful and specific. ✓

Weaknesses: Genre classification could be more precise (Action-Thriller/Superhero-Noir vs. Dystopian Action-Drama). Toronto not mentioned. 'Loyalty/family' not explicitly called out as a theme. Minor: confidence score of 1 seems overconfident.

Overall, this bible is detailed, specific, and could largely serve as a production reference document for this screenplay, with minor gaps.

Key quality criteria:
1. Is the logline specific to THIS screenplay — does it name The Mariner as protagonist, identify the gang/Salvatori as antagonist, and mention the building/floor stakes?
2. Does the synopsis cover the full arc: Mariner's vigilante crusade, Rose's revelation about stolen crypto, the father's dark truth, and the climactic confrontation?
3. Are the themes specific and grounded? Expect: father/legacy/disillusionment, identity/hero myth, loyalty/family, truth vs. comfortable lies.
4. Is the act structure accurate? The script has 3 acts roughly: setup of vigilante world → escalating gang conflict + Rose twist → climax on 15th floor + father revelation.
5. Is the protagonist journey correct? Mariner begins believing his father was a hero, discovers the man was abusive and alive, ends redefining heroism on his own terms.
6. Is the setting grounded in specifics? Expect: contemporary urban Toronto setting, the Ruddy & Greene Building (15th floor final confrontation), fishing-themed vigilante aesthetic.
7. Is the genre identified correctly? This is an Action-Thriller or Superhero-Noir — not drama or comedy.
8. Does the analysis avoid generic statements that could apply to any film?

Score strictly: 0.8+ only if the bible could serve as a production reference document for THIS specific screenplay.

</Rubric>"}]

prompt.raw:
You are a professional script reader and story analyst. Read the following screenplay and produce a comprehensive script bible.

SCREENPLAY:
==========
The Mariner

What happens when a hero’s origin story crumbles?  
FADE IN:

EXT. CITY CENTRE \- NIGHT

A ruined city. Fires burn here and there, some from barrels with huddled homeless homeless warming themselves, others from destroyed buildings. A fallen skyscraper has carved away chunks of its neighbours, filling an intersection with rubble. Gunshots and screams are heard. Sirens are far in the distance.

OPENING TITLE

In 2020 the pandemic swept the globe. Everyone who could work remotely did, hollowing out the skyscrapers. The cascading bank failures and commercial real estate collapse of 2025 turned these once pristine urban centres into sprawling no-go zones. Vigilanties arise to do what the police couldn’t.

EXT. RUDDY & GREENE BUILDING \- FRONT \- NIGHT

Classic 15-story sandstone building. Once beautiful, now riddled with bullet holes and graffiti. Parked outside are rows of high-end European SUVs.

EXT. RUDDY & GREENE BUILDING \- REAR \- NIGHT

Grimy alley. A delivery van rolls up. “McAngus Fish & Brewis” painted on the side with a giant fake fish on top.

THE MARINER, hidden in shadow, gets out of the van and flings open the building’s back door, his huge frame silhouetted against the interior’s light. He charges in. All we hear are the sounds: yells, violent fighting, silence. Heavy footsteps, DING, elevator doors open, muzak version of “Faith” by George Michael playing with Mariner humming along, elevator doors close.

As the elevator rises, one window lights up on each floor, all the way to the very top.

More sounds: DING, elevator opens, “Faith” playing, yelling, running, gunfire, screams, silence. Heavy footsteps. A door kicked in, more gunfire, fighting, wet cracking sounds, silence.

ROSE (O.S.)  
Billy?\!

MARINER (O.S.)  
Not today\! C’mon, we gotta go.

More sounds: two people running down a hallway, a single gunshot, THWACK, nothing. DING, elevator doors.

INT. RUDDY & GREEN BUILDING \- ELEVATOR

UB40's "I Can't Help Falling in Love With You" muzak.

ROSE (mid-30s) leans against the elevator wall, panting. She’s wearing a Tim Hortons uniform, her hair is a mess, and there’s blood spattered on her face.

She looks right, pants a few more times, then squints in disgust. She gingerly picks up a bloody scrap of something with hair on it. She snorts and drops it.

ROSE  
Is that skull??

Next to her is Mariner, also panting, covered in far more blood. His face is broad, stoic, and frowning. He has gray-blue eyes and a thick, curly, beard barely streaked with gray. He wears a red knit cap and a small mask.

FULL-SCREEN SUPERIMPOSED TITLE CARD: “THE MARINER”

He blinks a few times as a drop of blood falls from his eyebrow. He looks toward her, then spits out some blood.

MARINER  
Yeaaaaah...

He’s built like a pro-wrestler. Moss green SWEATER stretched over his huge frame, sleeves rolled showing heavy leather BRACERS adorned with anchors. His inner forearm has a classic heart-and-arrow TATTOO with “Dad” in the centre. Across his chest is a BANDOLIER of bright red 12-gauge shells, FISH HOOKS hanging from the bottom edge. Heavy rubberized BOOTS go past his knees. A CAPE of heavy FISHING NET edged with WEIGHTS hangs from his shoulders.

He holds a STOUT WOODEN OAR in both hands, edged with METAL and dripping with BLOOD. He shakes it to dislodge another GRISLY BIT which hits the ground with a wet plop.

ROSE  
How the hell did you find me?

Mariner looks down and mumbles something.

ROSE  
(annoyed)  
What?

MARINER  
AirTag.

ROSE  
What??

MARINER  
I stashed an AirTag in your purse. Last Thanksgiving at ma’s.

ROSE  
Shit\! My purse\!

MARINER  
Just in case\! I worry about--

ROSE  
Shut it\! We gotta go back for it.

MARINER  
What? No way\! I’m taking you home. It’s my fault they went after you. I never thought they’d--

ROSE  
(laughing)  
You think they’re after you?

Mariner looks confused. DING \- elevator doors open on 12\.

Three THUGS are waiting for them. THUG 1 raises his GUN... Mariner SWINGS HIS OAR in an arc, catching the gun... BANG \- shot goes high, GUN FLIES out of his hand... Mariner SIDE-KICKS him in the chest... Thug 1 FLIES back, knocking the others to the GROUND.

Rose has been frantically pressing the CLOSE DOOR button.

The thugs struggle to their feet.

THUG 2  
(into radio)  
We’ve got them on twelve.

THUGS 2 & 3 DRAW GUNS. BLAM BLAM BLAM. Rose and Mariner LEAP to the side... DOORS begin to CLOSE... Thug 2 LUNGES at the elevator BUTTON... Mariner’s OAR flashes through the closing doors... Thug 2 SCREAMS and CURSES... Doors close.

THUG  
(muffled)  
Go\! Go\! Down to eight\!

Rose presses 11\.

ROSE  
We’ll take the stairs up. They won’t expect that.

MARINER  
Because it’s stupid\!

Rose raises an eyebrow at Mariner’s crazy outfit.

MARINER  
It’s functional\! ... And an homage.

DING. Doors open. Rose peers out. Silence.

MARINER  
(whispering)  
Rose... No\!

Rose quietly darts out of the elevator. Mariner tries to grab her but misses. He hesitates... Doors start closing. Mariner dashes out after her, grumbling.

INT. 11TH FLOOR \- CONTINUOUS

They hunt for a stairwell through the dim corridors. Lights flicker, papers and office furniture litter the hallway.

ROSE  
Wait. Did you mean an homage to... dad? Are you ser--

MARINER  
Shh\!

He PULLS her into an EMPTY ROOM. They hide behind a toppled FILING CABINET and listen, catching their breath. Someone slowly walks by. Mariner looks down at his “Dad” TATTOO.

BEGIN FLASHBACK: EXT. COASTLINE \- DAY \- PAST

Young Mariner (8) and his dad (30s, fisherman) bob happily in a wooden sailboat near the shoreline. Golden light bathes them. Dad is behind Mariner, teaching him to cast. They laugh and smile, enjoying the day.

DAD  
Oi, me bosun. Ya knows what’s a hero? ‘E’s the one what’s brave an true. You remembers that. Go on now an fetch yer pa anudder.

Young Mariner nods, fetching a beer with the seriousness of a young boy with a job to do.

END FLASHBACK.

Rose nudges him, breaking his reverie. He grabs her hand and leads her out of the room. They find a stairwell.

INT. STAIRWELL \- CONTINUOUS

She goes up, he goes down.

MARINER  
You mad? We have to go\! I’ll buy you a new bloody purse.

ROSE  
It ain’t the purse. It’s... a blockchain password to my life savings. I hid it on a chip in the lining. It’s all I got, Billy\!

She gives a pleading look and heads up stairs. Mariner follows, grumbling.

INT. 12TH FLOOR STAIRWELL \- CONTINUOUS

MARINER  
The hell’s a blockchain?

BOOM \- A thug (ROSCO) opens the stairwell door, slamming Rose into Mariner. They stumble... Mariner YANKS Rosco into the stairwell... SMASHES his face into the wall... Rosco drops his GUN... Rose picks it up and points it at the back of his head as Mariner keeps him pressed into the wall.

ROSE  
Hey, Rosco. Explain blockchain.

ROSCO  
(panicked, face smooshed)  
What?\!

Rose cocks the gun.

ROSCO  
Okay, okay\! It’s, uh, a decentralized digital ledger detailing asset ownership. Untraceable. Oh man, Mr. Salvatori’s gonna kill ya, Rosie.

Rose nods “yes," maybe at both things. She motions for Mariner to do something to Rosco.

ROSCO  
He ain’t gonna let you walk with 20 mil--

Mariner cracks his head into the wall and Rosco crumples. Rose tucks the gun into her pants. Mariner stares at her.

MARINER  
Was he going to say 20 million? And how the hell do you know these assholes?

Rose starts up the stairs again. Mariner grabs her arm. She struggles. He lets her go. She scowls at him.

ROSE  
I stole it, okay? From these assholes. It’s gang money.

MARINER  
You got me involved in stealing gang money?

ROSE  
Who got you involved, Mr. Fucking AirTag?

Rose storms up the stairs and out of the door to the 13th floor. Mariner follows.

INT. 13TH FLOOR

VINNIE, MIKEY, and CARLOS patrol the ruined corridor.

MIKEY  
You heard about WaldoCoin?

VINNIE  
Man, that shit’s a scam. You gotta get into PonziCoin. My cousin’s in it. He says it’s legit.

They hear the stairwell door slam open on the other side of the floor and angry voices. Vinnie puts a finger to his lips and motions Mikey and Carlos to follow.

MARINER (O.S.)  
We’re leaving. Now. I don’t steal, Rose. It’s against my code.

The thugs creep down the hallway toward the voices.

ROSE (O.S.)  
(laughing but angry)  
From criminals? We take it, their gang collapses. And what code??

Vinnie motions the others to stop at the corner, gun ready.

MARINER (O.S.)  
I live by the Mariner’s code: honesty, bravery, and grit. A true hero never backs down from a fight.

ROSE (O.S.)  
You know who you sound like...

The thugs appear at the end of the hallway, guns raised.

CARLOS  
Freeze, assholes\! On the ground\!

VINNIE  
Rosey, baby. We don’t wanna hurt ya. Mr. Salvatori just wants his password. That’s all. Then maybe, if you’re reeeaal nice, maybe you can be my booty call some more.

Rose’s eyes narrow. Mariner scowls and steps in front.

MIKEY  
Hey, Finding Nemo, drop the piece.

VINNIE cackles. Mainer slowly unholsters his weapon \- a fat, bright-orange pistol.

MIKEY  
Is that a... flare gun?

The thugs all start laughing. POP \- Blinding red flare streaks out... Mariner spins, broad back protecting Rose... Flare hits VINNIE in the chest. He SCREAMS and drops, still screaming. BLAM-BLAM-BLAM \- Thugs fire... One shot hits Mariner’s back... He shoves Rose through an open door...

Rose sees the stark red light of the flare and hears VINNIE’S screams as it continues to burn. Mariner lunges toward the other thugs. BLAM-BLAM. THUMP. GRUNT. CRACK. SCREAM. BLAM. Rose peeks out. Mariner is holding Mikey and Carlos by the necks, a foot off the ground. CRUNCH \- he cracks their heads together and they drop.

Rose joins Mariner as he wipes his BLOODY OAR on Carlos’ back. “BOSUN” is carved into the wood. Rose gives Vinnie’s body a kick.

ROSE  
Prick. Oh shit\! You’re bleeding.

She rips off a strip of her uniform and wraps it around Mariner’s leg. She eyes the holes in his sweater.

MARINER  
I hate your boyfriend.

ROSE  
Ex boyfriend. Yeah I know. Vinnie’s unique... charm convinced me that robbing these jackasses was the right move.

MARINER  
He convinced me, too. C’mon.

INT. 15TH FLOOR

This floor isn’t destroyed like the others. It’s CLEAN, LUXURIOUS, and DIMLY LIT, like an old nightclub. Mariner’s opening fight is evident: pooled blood and gouged walls.

The stairwell door opens. Mariner and Rose edge into the corridor. They pass a number of ROOMS: pool table, cigar lounge, a smashed door with a chair and ropes on the floor.

At the end of the hallway they stop at a sturdy closed door. Rose nods. Mariner checks the handle. Locked.

Mariner shoves his OAR into the doorframe and strains. CREAK. He bears down harder, veins rising on his neck, muscles bulging. CREAK. CRACK. CRASH \- It splinters open.

They survey the room: huge wooden DESK atop a thick Persian RUG, BOOKSHELVES of leather-bound books, fine BOOZE fills a glass case, mismatched PAINTINGS of different periods, and a bowl of MINTS on the desk. Next to that, a PURSE.

Rose tiptoes in and picks up her PURSE as Mariner moves to check two SIDE DOORS.

ROSE  
Christ you’re strong, Billy. That’s gotta be juice, right?

BEGIN FLASHBACK: EXT. BACKYARD \- DAY \- PAST

The warm sun dapples through trees in the idyllic yard, a trim house with a homemade pie in the window. Young Mariner’s dad patiently explains the basics of weightlifting. Mariner struggles to bench press, dad encouraging and helping him. He makes it. They laugh. Dad puts his arm around him and tousles his hair.

DAD  
Yer gonna be a scrapper, ya knows that? Jus remember, me bosun, don’t never run from a fight. Not again, ya hears me? It’s not what a man does. It’s not what a MacAngus does. Let’s do a round.

Mariner nods, lips pursed in resolve. He has a black eye.

END FLASHBACK.

MARINER  
Just hours of pumpin’ tin, sis. Just like dad taught me.

ROSE  
You’re serious about this dad shit? You talking about the summer he took you to buddy’s place?

MARINER  
(through gritted teeth)  
Dad taught me everything. Sailing, fishing, fighting. How to get strong and be brave. How to be a man. Everything. It broke me when he died.

ROSE  
Died\!?

Rose turns to Mariner.

ROSE (CONT’D)  
Seriously? All these years you thought he was dead? Is that what you told yourself? Is that why you thought he never came to see you? 

MARINER  
What are you saying?

ROSE  
Christ, Billy, he’s living up in  Chimney Bay with some skank. He was on the pogey that summer at buddy’s\! He was supposed to be taking care of you. Instead he’d ditch you in his deadbeat friend’s yard all summer while they got shit faced.

MARINER  
(quietly)  
Bullshit. Ma would’a said.

ROSE  
You were too young, Will. She didn’t want to break your heart.

Mariner looks lost; a hulking bearded man with the eyes of a wounded child.

TINKLE \- ICE in a TUMBLER of scotch melts. As they look at the desk, they notice a STILL-BURNING CIGAR.

A VOICE booms from the door they entered through.

SALVATORI (O.S.)  
(Russian accent)  
Rosey\! Somehow I just knew you’d be back.

Two THUGS burst in through the office doors, GUNS raised. Rose retreats behind the desk. Mariner stares vacantly.

SALVATORI enters, holding a SUBMACHINE GUN. He’s WHITE FROM HEAD TO TOE: white silk suit, tie, shirt, shoes. White hair pulled back into a small ponytail, and a finely groomed white MOUSTACHE. His red-tinted glasses stand out in contrast. He wears a thick hoop earring, gold rings on each finger, and has a single GOLDEN TOOTH.

Salvatori strides confidently up to Rose, submachine gun levelled at her gut. He never breaks eye contact with her.

SALVATORI  
You brought a big man with you.

EXT. BACKYARD \- DAY \- (FLASHBACK)

The yard is overgrown with WEEDS, DISCARDED BOTTLES, some RUSTY WEIGHTS, and a BENCH PRESS repaired with peeling duct-tape. Young Mariner’s dad PUNCHES him in the face.

DAD  
Jeeeeezuz,  b'y, ya gots to stand up fer yerself, bosun\! No wonders ya got yer ass whupped. Hands up, now\! Don’t never run from a fight again, ya hear?

Mariner nods, lips pursed in resolve. Dad PUNCHES him again.

DAD  
Fer fuck sakes\! Yer a bag a sad. Go lift some tin till yer a man.

Dad heads into the house in DISGUST, kicking a BOTTLE which SMASHES against the stucco. Mariner, wiping a split lip, goes to the BENCH PRESS and struggles to add some weights.

INT. STAIRWELL \- (BACK TO PRESENT)

SALVATORI  
Who are you, big man?

MARINER  
(quietly)  
Nobody.

SALVATORI  
You certainly look like you’re trying to be somebody. Like a big fucking hero.

EXT. COASTLINE \- DAY \- (FLASHBACK)

Young Mariner in a boat, the bottom littered with empty beer bottles. Dad is pissing off the side. Mariner is under-dressed and shivering.

DAD  
Ya know whats a hero, b’y? It’s got two thumbs and ‘e looks like this\! Ha\! Now fetch me anudder. An don’t gimme no sass abouts it like yer mudder.

EXT. BACKYARD \- DAY \- (FLASHBACK)

SALVATORI (CONT’D)  
So? Are you a big fucking hero?

MARINER  
(quietly)  
No.

The thugs take Mariner’s OAR and kick the back of his legs so he’s kneeling at their feet. Mariner doesn’t resist.

BLAM. Salvatori fires a SHOT into the bookcase behind Rose. Mariner flinches and Rose stifles a scream.

SALVATORI  
Memory stick, Rosey.

Rose swallows.

ROSE  
He’s no hero. He’s a gullible ass who spent his entire life in the shadow of a loser.

Mariner closes his eyes and hangs his head. Salvatori smirks at this.

ROSE  
I know a hero, though. He sees the best in people even if they don’t deserve it. He grew up in a shitty home with a shitty father who kicked the crap out of him and then abandoned him for some junkie. And instead of following that path, he forged his own.

Mariner looks up at Rose, eyes moist.

SALVATORI  
Okay, okay, shut up now.

ROSE  
He’s a model of honesty and bravery and grit. He somehow turned a deadbeat father into a superhero. Then became a real one.

Salvatori points his GUN at Rose’s head.

SALVATORI  
(screaming)  
PASSWORD, ROSE. NOW\!

ROSE  
And he never backs down from a fight.

A smile crosses Mariner’s face as he clenches his hands into fists.

CUT TO BLACK.
==========

Analyze the screenplay above and extract:

1. **title** — The title of the screenplay.
2. **logline** — A single compelling sentence that captures the story's premise, protagonist, conflict, and stakes.
3. **synopsis** — A 1-3 paragraph synopsis covering the full story arc. Include key plot points but keep it concise.
4. **act_structure** — Break the screenplay into its dramatic acts (typically 3, but use whatever structure the script supports). For each act:
   - act_number (1, 2, 3...)
   - title (short label like "Setup", "Confrontation", "Resolution")
   - start_scene (the scene heading or description where the act begins)
   - end_scene (where the act ends)
   - summary (1-2 sentences)
   - turning_points (key plot beats within the act)
5. **themes** — Major thematic concerns. For each:
   - theme (name)
   - description (how it manifests)
   - evidence (scene references or brief quotes, 2-4 items)
6. **narrative_arc** — Describe the overall story shape and dramatic movement.
7. **genre** — Primary genre (e.g. "Action-Thriller", "Drama", "Sci-Fi Horror").
8. **tone** — Dominant tone (e.g. "Gritty and intense", "Darkly comedic").
9. **protagonist_journey** — The protagonist's transformation arc.
10. **central_conflict** — The core dramatic conflict.
11. **setting_overview** — Time period, locations, and world context.
12. **confidence** — Your confidence in this analysis (0.0-1.0).

Return ONLY valid JSON matching this schema. Be specific and evidence-grounded — name actual characters, locations, and scenes. Do not write generic analysis that could apply to any film.

SCREENPLAY:
==========
{{screenplay}}
==========

Analyze the screenplay above and extract:

1. **title** — The title of the screenplay.
2. **logline** — A single compelling sentence that captures the story's premise, protagonist, conflict, and stakes.
3. **synopsis** — A 1-3 paragraph synopsis covering the full story arc. Include key plot points but keep it concise.
4. **act_structure** — Break the screenplay into its dramatic acts (typically 3, but use whatever structure the script supports). For each act:
   - act_number (1, 2, 3...)
   - title (short label like "Setup", "Confrontation", "Resolution")
   - start_scene (the scene heading or description where the act begins)
   - end_scene (where the act ends)

   - turning_points (key plot beats within the act)
5. **themes** — Major thematic concerns. For each:
   - theme (name)
   - description (how it manifests)
   - evidence (scene references or brief quotes, 2-4 items)
6. **narrative_arc** — Describe the overall story shape and dramatic movement.
7. **genre** — Primary genre (e.g. "Action-Thriller", "Drama", "Sci-Fi Horror").
8. **tone** — Dominant tone (e.g. "Gritty and intense", "Darkly comedic").
9. **protagonist_journey** — The protagonist's transformation arc.

{
  "title": "The Mariner",
  "logline": "In a crumbling dystopian city, a nautical-themed vigilante rescues his sister from a ruthless mob boss, only to discover that his heroic 'Mariner's Code' is a delusion built on a lifetime of lies about his abusive father.",
  "synopsis": "Set in 2025 following a global economic collapse, the story follows Billy (The Mariner), a hulking vigilante who uses fishing-themed weaponry to navigate the 'no-go zones' of a ruined city. Billy infiltrates the Ruddy & Greene building to rescue his sister, Rose, from a gang led by the Russian mobster Salvatori. As they fight their way up the skyscraper, Billy reveals he is motivated by a 'Mariner's Code' of honor passed down by his heroic fisherman father.\n\nHowever, the mission takes a turn when Rose reveals she wasn't just a victim; she stole $20 million in blockchain currency from the gang. More devastatingly, she shatters Billy's worldview by revealing that their father wasn't a hero who died at sea, but an abusive alcoholic who is still alive and living with a mistress. Billy's 'origin story' is revealed to be a protective lie told by his mother.\n\nTrapped in Salvatori’s luxury penthouse, Billy is initially broken by the revelation and surrenders to the mob. However, Rose delivers a powerful monologue, reframing Billy's heroism: he is a hero not because of his father, but because he chose to be a good man despite his father's cruelty. Reinvigorated by this new truth, Billy prepares to fight Salvatori and his men as the screen cuts to black.",
  "act_structure": [
    {
      "act_number": 1,
      "title": "The Extraction",
      "start_scene": "EXT. CITY CENTRE - NIGHT",
      "end_scene": "INT. RUDDY & GREEN BUILDING - ELEVATOR",
      "summary": "The Mariner infiltrates a gang-controlled skyscraper to rescue his sister, Rose, establishing his brutal combat style and nautical persona.",
      "turning_points": [
        "The Mariner uses an AirTag to track Rose to the Ruddy & Greene building.",
        "The 'The Mariner' title card reveals Billy's full vigilante regalia (oar, fishnet cape, shell bandolier).",
        "Rose reveals she isn't just a hostage; she has a specific agenda involving her purse."
      ]
    },
    {
      "act_number": 2,
      "title": "The Ascent and the Revelation",
      "start_scene": "INT. 11TH FLOOR - CONTINUOUS",
      "end_scene": "INT. 15TH FLOOR (Office)",
      "summary": "As they climb the building, the truth about the mission and Billy's past comes to light, culminating in the destruction of Billy's heroic mythology.",
      "turning_points": [
        "Rose admits she stole $20 million in gang money, complicating Billy's 'heroic' rescue.",
        "Flashbacks contrast Billy's idealized memories of his father with the gritty reality of his training.",
        "Rose reveals the truth: their father is a 'deadbeat' living in Chimney Bay, not a deceased hero."
      ]
    },
    {
      "act_number": 3,
      "title": "The True Hero",
      "start_scene": "INT. 15TH FLOOR (Salvatori's Entrance)",
      "end_scene": "INT. 15TH FLOOR (Final Cut to Black)",
      "summary": "Confronted by Salvatori, a broken Billy finds a new sense of purpose through Rose's validation of his self-made character.",
      "turning_points": [
        "Salvatori corners the siblings, and Billy, disillusioned, drops his weapon and kneels.",
        "A final flashback reveals the father was physically abusive and dismissive.",
        "Rose's speech redefines Billy as a hero for forging his own path, prompting him to clench his fists for the final battle."
      ]
    }
  ],
  "themes": [
    {
      "theme": "The Deconstruction of Heroism",
      "description": "The script explores the gap between the 'superhero' mythos and the trauma that often fuels it.",
      "evidence": [
        "Billy's 'Mariner's Code' (honesty, bravery, grit) is contrasted with the reality of his father's abuse.",
        "Rose's line: 'He somehow turned a deadbeat father into a superhero. Then became a real one.'"
      ]
    },
    {
      "theme": "Subjective Memory vs. Objective Truth",
      "description": "The narrative uses flashbacks to show how Billy sanitized his childhood trauma to survive.",
      "evidence": [
        "The first flashback shows a 'golden light' sailboat scene, while the later flashback shows the same setting littered with beer bottles and violence.",
        "Rose's revelation that their mother lied to protect Billy's heart."
      ]
    },
    {
      "theme": "Economic Desperation",
      "description": "The setting of a collapsed real estate market and 'WaldoCoin' scams drives the plot's stakes.",
      "evidence": [
        "The opening crawl regarding the 2025 commercial real estate collapse.",
        "The thugs arguing about 'PonziCoin' and 'WaldoCoin' while patrolling."
      ]
    }
  ],
  "narrative_arc": "The story follows a vertical trajectory—both physically, as the characters ascend the skyscraper, and psychologically, as Billy descends from a state of delusional idealism into a painful but empowering reality.",
  "genre": "Dystopian Action-Drama",
  "tone": "Gritty and visceral, with a touch of surrealism due to the protagonist's eccentric nautical costume.",
  "protagonist_journey": "Billy begins as a man living in the shadow of a fictionalized father, using a 'code' to justify his violence. By the end, he loses his false identity but gains a true one, realizing his strength comes from his own resilience rather than his lineage.",
  "central_conflict": "Internal: Billy's struggle to reconcile his heroic identity with the truth of his abusive past. External: Billy and Rose's attempt to escape Salvatori's gang with the stolen $20 million.",
  "setting_overview": "A dystopian 2025 'City Centre' characterized by 'no-go zones' and hollowed-out skyscrapers following a pandemic and economic collapse. The primary location is the Ruddy & Greene building, a once-luxurious sandstone office tower now serving as a gang fortress.",
  "confidence": 1.0
}

parts.text:
{
  "title": "The Mariner",

More devastatingly, she shatters Billy's worldview by revealing that their father wasn't a hero who died at sea, but an abusive alcoholic who is still alive and living with a mistress. Billy's 'origin story' is revealed to be a protective lie told by his mother.\n\nTrapped in Salvatori’s luxury penthouse, Billy is initially broken by the revelation and surrenders to the mob. However, Rose delivers a powerful monologue, reframing Billy's heroism: he is a hero not because of his father, but because he chose to be a good man despite his father's cruelty. Reinvigorated by this new truth, Billy prepares to fight Salvatori and his men as the screen cuts to black.",
